title: Left Panel Resize Handle Stability and Live Collapse Behavior

changes: Removed hover translateX behavior from the layer rail handle.
Removed collapsed-state right-offset variation so the handle keeps the same anchor position.
Kept handle width, height, padding, border width, and transform stable on hover.
Hover/focus now only changes opacity, border color, background/shadow, and icon color.

approach: Added transient drag preview state for expanded/collapsed rail rendering.
Crossing below the collapse threshold switches the rendered rail to the slim collapsed version during drag.
Dragging back above the threshold restores the expanded rail preview before pointer release.
Only threshold crossings update React state; per-pixel width changes continue through the CSS variable.
Final collapsed or expanded state is committed on pointerup.
live_collapse_preview.previous_expanded_width_behavior: Preserved via cfs.layerRailWidth localStorage and restored on expand.

performance_safeguards: No backend, PostGIS, API, data pipeline, parcel search, or SceneView data layer changes.
Drag width still updates with requestAnimationFrame and direct CSS variable writes.
React state updates are limited to drag start, drag end, and expanded/collapsed preview threshold changes.
A dragging class disables rail transition durations during active drag.

validation_results.backend_dev_log_new_errors: none found beyond launcher redirection noise

smoke_test_results.click_handle_expands: passed; rail returned to expanded state
smoke_test_results.drag_left_collapses: passed; rail became 64px and retained Layers label
smoke_test_results.drag_right_expands: passed; rail expanded to 512px in browser smoke test
smoke_test_results.fullscreen_map_mode: passed; right intelligence panel and footer hidden, left rail and handle remained available
smoke_test_results.selected_parcel_school_flood_development: API health checks passed and related code paths were not modified

remaining_limitations: The in-app browser API cannot pause in the middle of a drag gesture, so live collapse preview was verified by source behavior and final drag states rather than a mid-drag screenshot.
ArcGIS draped label placement warnings may still appear from existing map graphics and are unrelated to this resize follow-up.
